name: Tiramisu Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 24 oz Cream cheese or Neufchatel cheese, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Crust: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Butter an 8-inch springform pan. Stir in butter and espresso powder in a small bowl until combined. Stir in crumbs until crumbs are evenly moistened. Pat evenly over bottom of prepared pan. Bake for 10 minutes. Cool on a wire rack. Keep the oven on. Tightly cover the outside bottom and sides of the springform pan with heavy-duty foil.
Filling: Meanwhile, beat cream cheese and mascarpone in a large mixer bowl at medium-high speed until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes. Gradually beat in sugar, scraping down the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula, until completely smooth, about 3 minutes. Reduce speed to medium and beat in vanilla and salt. Add eggs one at a time, beating just until blended after each addition. Pour filling over crust in prepared pan and place in a large roasting pan. Dissolve espresso in hot water. Fold into the remaining filling with brandy and grated chocolate. Spoon mixture evenly over filling in pan; smooth top with a spatula. Place pan on oven rack. Pour enough hot water into the roasting pan to come 1 inch up the side of the springform pan. Bake for 1-1/4 hours or until center is just set. Remove cheesecake from water bath. Cool completely on a wire rack. Remove foil. Cover and refrigerate overnight. Just before serving, run a knife around the edge of the pan; remove sides. Sprinkle lightly with cocoa. Makes 12 servings.
